metadata.summary: A client to provide LLM responses for FutureHouse applications.

documentation:
[![Python 3.11+](https://img.shields.io/badge/python-3.11+-blue.svg)]()

<!-- [![tests](https://github.com/Future-House/llm-client/actions/workflows/test.yaml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/Future-House/llm-client) -->

[![PyPI version](https://badge.fury.io/py/fh-llm-client.svg)](https://badge.fury.io/py/fh-llm-client)

# llm-client

Central FutureHouse LLM client library.

## Quick Start

```
$ pip install -e .
$ uv sync && uv run pytest -n auto
```

## Clients

- LLMModel
- LLMResult

## Examples

- [PaperQA](https://github.com/Future-House/paper-qa/compare/main...llm-result-client)
- [LDP](https://github.com/Future-House/ldp/compare/main...llm-result-client)

documentation: Raised when the completion does not match the specified schema.

documentation:
Filter out common parsing failures not worth looking into from logs.

Returns:
    False if the LogRecord should be filtered out, otherwise True to keep it.
Error to throw if some aspect of a Message variant is malformed.

modules.name: llmclient.version
documentation: file generated by setuptools_scm
don't change, don't track in version control

methods.name: llmclient.messages.Message.append_text
documentation:
Append text to the content.

Args:
    text: The text to append.
    delim: The delimiter to use when concatenating strings.
    inplace: Whether to modify the message in place.

Returns:
    The modified message. Note that the original message is modified and returned
    if `inplace=True` and a new message is returned otherwise.

classes.name: llmclient.types.LLMResult

documentation: Return the cost of the result in dollars.

methods.name: llmclient.types.LLMResult.provider
documentation: Get the model provider's name (e.g. "openai", "mistral").
TODO: These two methods were implemented in ldp, but not in pqa.
TODO: Check if they're necessary

methods.name: llmclient.types.LLMResult.get_supported_openai_params
documentation: Get the supported OpenAI parameters for the model.

A class to hold the result of a LLM completion.

To associate a group of LLMResults, you can use the `set_llm_session_ids` context manager:

```python
my_session_id = uuid4()
with set_llm_session_ids(my_session_id):
    # code that generates LLMResults
    pass
```

and all the LLMResults generated within the context will have the same `session_id`.
This can be combined with LLMModels `llm_result_callback` to store all LLMResults.

variables.name: llmclient.rate_limiter.UNKNOWN_IP
documentation: noqa: S104

classes.name: llmclient.rate_limiter.GlobalRateLimiter

methods.name: llmclient.rate_limiter.GlobalRateLimiter.parse_namespace_and_primary_key
documentation:
Turn namespace_and_key tuple into a namespace and primary-key.

If using a namespace starting with "get", then the primary key will be url parsed.
"get" namespaces will also have their machine_ids appended to the namespace here,
unless the primary key is in the NO_MACHINE_ID_EXTENSIONS list, in which case
the outbound IP will be used.

methods.name: llmclient.rate_limiter.GlobalRateLimiter.parse_rate_limits_and_namespace
documentation:
Get rate limit and new namespace for a given namespace and primary_key.

This parsing logic finds the correct rate limits for a namespace/primary_key.
It's a bit complex due to the <MATCH ALL> and <MATCH MACHINE ID> placeholders.
These allow users to match

methods.name: llmclient.rate_limiter.GlobalRateLimiter.parse_key
documentation:
Parse the rate limit item from a redis/in-memory key.

Args:
    key (str): is created with RateLimitItem.key_for(*identifiers),
    the first key is the namespace, then the next two will be our identifiers.

methods.name: llmclient.rate_limiter.GlobalRateLimiter.get_rate_limit_keys
documentation: Returns a list of current RateLimitItems with tuples of namespace and primary key.

methods.name: llmclient.rate_limiter.GlobalRateLimiter.get_in_memory_limit_keys
documentation: Returns a list of current RateLimitItems with tuples of namespace and primary key.

methods.name: llmclient.rate_limiter.GlobalRateLimiter.try_acquire
documentation:
Returns when the limit is satisfied for the namespace_and_key.

Args:
: namespace_and_key ({obj}`tuple[str, str]`): is

  : composed of a tuple with namespace (e.g. "get") and a primary-key
    (e.g. "arxiv.org"). namespaces can be nested with multiple '|',
    primary-keys in the "get" namespace will be stripped to the domain.

  rate_limit ({obj}`RateLimitItem | str | None`, optional): Optional

  : RateLimitItem to be used for the namespace and primary-key.
    If not provided, RATE_CONFIG will be used to find the rate limit.
    Can also use a string of the form:
    [count] [per|/] [n (optional)] [second|minute|hour|day|month|year]

  machine_id ({obj}`int`, optional): will be used to modify the namespace

  : of GET requests if the primary key is not in the
    NO_MACHINE_ID_EXTENSIONS list. In that case, the outbound IP will be
    used to modify the namespace.

  acquire_timeout ({obj}`float`, optional): is the maximum time (in seconds) to

  : wait for the rate limit to be satisfied.

  weight ({obj}`int`, optional): is the cost of the request,

  : default is 1. (could be tokens for example)

  raise_impossible_limits ({obj}`bool`, optional): flag will raise a

  : ValueError for weights that exceed the rate.

Raises:

: TimeoutError: if the acquire_timeout is exceeded.
  ValueError: if the weight exceeds the rate limit and raise_impossible_limits is True.

class_variables.name: llmclient.rate_limiter.GlobalRateLimiter.WAIT_INCREMENT
documentation: seconds

Rate limiter for all requests within or between processes.

Supports both Redis and in-memory storage.
'Global' refers to being able to limit the rate
of requests across processes with Redis.

functions.name: llmclient.embeddings.embedding_model_factory
documentation:
Factory function to create an appropriate EmbeddingModel based on the embedding string.

Supports:
- SentenceTransformer models prefixed with "st-" (e.g., "st-multi-qa-MiniLM-L6-cos-v1")
- LiteLLM models (default if no prefix is provided)
- Hybrid embeddings prefixed with "hybrid-", contains a sparse and a dense model

Args:
    embedding: The embedding model identifier. Supports prefixes like "st-" for SentenceTransformer
               and "hybrid-" for combining multiple embedding models.
    **kwargs: Additional keyword arguments for the embedding model.

classes.name: llmclient.embeddings.EmbeddingModes
documentation: Enum representing the different modes of an embedding model.

methods.name: llmclient.embeddings.EmbeddingModel.set_mode
documentation: Several embedding models have a 'mode' or prompt which affects output.

methods.name: llmclient.embeddings.LiteLLMEmbeddingModel._truncate_if_large
documentation: Truncate texts if they are too large by using litellm cost map.

classes.name: llmclient.embeddings.SparseEmbeddingModel
documentation: This is a very simple keyword search model - probably best to be mixed with others.

classes.name: llmclient.embeddings.SentenceTransformerEmbeddingModel

methods.name: llmclient.embeddings.SentenceTransformerEmbeddingModel.embed_documents
documentation:
Asynchronously embed a list of documents using SentenceTransformer.

Args:
    texts: A list of text documents to embed.

Returns:
    A list of embedding vectors.
An embedding model using SentenceTransformers.

functions.name: llmclient.llms.sum_logprobs
documentation:
Calculate the sum of the log probabilities of an LLM completion (a Choices object).

Args:
    choice: A sequence of choices from the completion.

Returns:
    The sum of the log probabilities of the choice.

functions.name: llmclient.llms.validate_json_completion
documentation:
Validate a completion against a JSON schema.

Args:
    completion: The completion to validate.
    output_type: The Pydantic model to validate the completion against.

functions.name: llmclient.llms.rate_limited
documentation: Decorator to rate limit relevant methods of an LLMModel.

methods.name: llmclient.llms.LLMModel.acomplete
documentation: Return the completion as string and the number of tokens in the prompt and completion.

methods.name: llmclient.llms.LLMModel.acomplete_iter
documentation:
Return an async generator that yields chunks of the completion.

Only the last tuple will be non-zero.

methods.name: llmclient.llms.LLMModel.achat
documentation: Return the completion as string and the number of tokens in the prompt and completion.

methods.name: llmclient.llms.LLMModel.achat_iter
documentation:
Return an async generator that yields chunks of the completion.

Only the last tuple will be non-zero.

methods.name: llmclient.llms.LLMModel._run_chat
documentation:
Run a chat prompt.

Args:
    prompt: Prompt to use.
    data: Keys for the input variables that will be formatted into prompt.
    callbacks: Optional functions to call with each chunk of the completion.
    name: Optional name for the result.
    system_prompt: System prompt to use, or None/empty string to not use one.

Returns:
    Result of the chat.

methods.name: llmclient.llms.LLMModel._run_completion
documentation:
Run a completion prompt.

Args:
    prompt: Prompt to use.
    data: Keys for the input variables that will be formatted into prompt.
    callbacks: Optional functions to call with each chunk of the completion.
    name: Optional name for the result.
    system_prompt: System prompt to use, or None/empty string to not use one.

Returns:
    Result of the completion.

classes.name: llmclient.llms.PassThroughRouter
documentation: Router that is just a wrapper on LiteLLM's normal free functions.

classes.name: llmclient.llms.LiteLLMModel

methods.name: llmclient.llms.LiteLLMModel.maybe_set_config_attribute
documentation: If a user only gives a name, make a sensible config dict for them.

methods.name: llmclient.llms.LiteLLMModel.select_tool
documentation: Shim to aviary.core.ToolSelector that supports tool schemae.
A wrapper around the litellm library.

classes.name: llmclient.llms.MultipleCompletionLLMModel
documentation: Run n completions at once, all starting from the same messages.
